description.p: LNXlink is a Linux companion app that seamlessly integrates your system with external applications like Home Assistant. It uses MQTT, a lightweight messaging protocol, to enable real-time data exchange and remote control capabilities.

ul.li: Sensors: Automatically discover sensors that monitor and control the system.
Home Assistant: Uses MQTT Autodiscovery to create entities and shows if update is required.
No sudo required: No need to be root user to install and use, unless used on server setup.
Easily expanded: Any new module is automatically imported and custom modules can be added.
Fingerprint scanner module
Include/exclude options for interfaces module
Configuration option for a custom logging directory
Compatibility with older loginctl JSON output
Excluded entries are now automatically deleted from Home Assistant
dasbus dependency dropped
asyncio standard library requirement dropped
Build dependencies bumped for better stability
MQTT: Connect through the Home Assistant API - great for Nabu Casa users or anyone who doesn't want to expose their MQTT broker
LWT Sensor: Track when your device goes offline with the new availability sensor
Current User: New sensor showing who is currently logged in
Update Entities: New button to manually refresh all Home Assistant entities
Systemd: Run lnxlink as a user service
Bash: New select entity type - expose a dropdown driven by a shell command
Bash: Set device_class and state_class on custom sensors for better HA integration
Notifications: Use custom text IDs to update or close notifications by name
Add interactive module selection, watch changes, clipboard support, mouse scrolling, and an Arch Linux AUR package.
Improve Wayland support for brightness, screenshots, active window, fullscreen, and screen control modules.
Fix unsupported module startup, media playback stalls, disk I/O filtering, and multi-key send keys handling.
Add advanced mouse input, Wayland input support, improved notifications, non-retainable MQTT topics, and Steam library configuration.
Relax keyboard hotkey configuration and improve module initialization error handling.
Fix Docker controls, hotkeys, battery discovery names, and MQTT environment variable credentials.
Add Docker update entities, topic cleanup on shutdown, autodiscovery prefix configuration, and detailed disk usage options.
Improve LWT updates, sensor availability behavior, and media playback titles.
Fix play media, screen control, and invalid MQTT topic characters.
Add device tracking, BeaconDB Wi-Fi tracking, extra UPower battery attributes, and development Docker builds.
Replace Dasbus with Jeepney and improve system monitor stability.
Fix Bluetooth output parsing and Disk IO initialization.
Add Wake On LAN, Wayland idle support, Bluetooth battery attributes, Docker suspend support, and module-defined settings.
Improve PipeWire audio selection, battery filtering, media volume control, and dependency updates.
Fix Docker integration, MQTT queue startup, boot selection, system updates, Bluetooth wildcard names, and blocking sudo commands.
Add Docker GUI app support, environment variables for sensitive values, configurable bash command timeouts, and logging level control.
Replace dasbus with jeepney, support off bash checks, and split core code into multiple files.
Fix MQTT reconnects, system updates in non-English locales, module discovery updates, and Bluetooth and Steam modules.
Add support for multiple media players and Arch installation.
Fix battery names with special characters and GI dependency requirements.
Add SystemD use inside flatpak with DBUS
Defaults configuration to ~/Documents/LNXlink/config.yaml
Defaults configuration to ~/Documents/lnxlink.yaml
Add Docker containers
Easy installation
